topic: Faros AI Review 2026: Measure the Real ROI of AI Coding Tools

key_points: Jellyfish focuses on DevFinOps and executive reporting with patented allocations model
Strong on resource allocation, planning, and investment insights for C-suite
Often takes about 9 months to reach ROI; covers fewer integrations but deeper planning
Industry's largest customer dataset but no multi-tool AI visibility or code-level insight
LinearB combines analytics with gitStream policy-as-code engine for PR automation
Supports workflow improvement and DORA metrics but lacks AI impact analysis
Charges per contributor; requires 2-4 weeks setup time
More automation-focused than Faros; acts on data rather than just displaying it
GetDX positions as AI-native developer analytics with developer experience focus
Emphasizes developer sentiment surveys alongside quantitative metrics
Lighter integration footprint but faster time-to-value than enterprise platforms
Less enterprise-ready than Faros for complex multi-system environments
Gitrecap/Plandek offer simpler DORA metrics dashboards for smaller teams
Lower price point but limited AI ROI measurement capabilities
DORA-focused without the broader SDLC data integration Faros provides
Exceeds AI is an emerging competitor targeting AI-native engineering intelligence
Focus on AI coding assistant evaluation and developer productivity benchmarking
Newer platform with less enterprise track record but strong AI-first positioning

stats.fact: Faros AI's 2026 AI Engineering Report synthesizes two years of telemetry from 22,000 developers across more than 4,000 teams
Task completion per developer is up 34% under high AI adoption; epics completed per developer up 66%
60% of AI-generated code is now being accepted into codebases, up from 20% a year earlier
Bugs per developer are up 54% and the incident-to-PR ratio has more than tripled under high AI adoption
Median PR review time has grown 5x and 31% more PRs are now merging without any review at all
Top US Bank case study: 20%+ increase in throughput and 15% reduction in cycle time across 1,000 developers
Microsoft Azure partnership case study: $10M in first-year ROI, 95% improvement in lead times, 72% boost in code quality
Faros AI pricing starts at $29 per contributor; modular pricing allows purchasing individual intelligence modules
84% of developers use AI tools that now write 41% of all code in 2026
Healthy ROI on AI coding tools is 2.5-3.5x average and 4-6x top quartile when full costs are measured
Developers on teams with high AI adoption complete 21% more tasks and merge 98% more PRs, but PR review time increases 91%
Example ROI calculation: 50 developers on Max plans at $120K/year; 8,400 PRs vs 5,200 baseline; $37.50 cost per incremental PR; 4:1 ROI if each PR saves 2 developer hours at $75/hour
